reason: The statement applies because the children of Israel were in Egypt under harsh conditions, being made to serve with rigor and facing affliction under a new king who enslaved them. The text also mentions the Hebrew midwives and the birth of Moses, who was later brought up in the house of Pharaoh's daughter. This indicates a situation of captivity and foreign rule, as well as the presence of key figures like Moses, who would later play a leadership role among the Israelites.
